web.title: Ko-fi vs Buy me a coffee - Which one is better? - YouTube
web.description: Are you trying to decide which online donations platform to use? I will compare Ko-fi vs “buy me a coffee” in just three minutes!
web.markdown:
![Thumbnail (1920x1080)](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/Za41PxSa9vw/maxresdefault.jpg)
# [Ko-fi vs Buy me a coffee - Which one is better?](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Za41PxSa9vw)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Clicks to Clients](https://www.youtube.com/@tipswithpunch)
**Uploaded at**: 2021-10-04
**Published at**: 
**Length**: 03:21
**Views**: 40915
**Likes**: 986
**Category**: Howto & Style

## Description

```
Are you trying to decide which online donations platform to use?
I will compare Ko-fi vs “buy me a coffee” in just three minutes!
So you can start receiving donations to fuel your passion, today!

Kofi: https://ko-fi.com?tipswithpunch-youtube
Buy me a coffee: https://buymeacoffee.com/?via=tipswithpunch

00:00 Video in summary

00:19 Features
So both of these services come with an option for one-time donations.
And it’s super easy to donate with both, supporters don’t even need an account to leave few bucks for a coffee.

On top of that, both offer a membership option. Memberships just mean you get recurring donations from your supporters. Usually, creators offer unique content for the members. So pretty much what people do on Patreon.

However, Ko-fi membership only comes with the paid plan. And Buy me a coffee offers it for free but they take a five percent fee from every donation.

Both offer a way to sell digital products & services. In addition, with Ko-fi you can also sell physical stuff. In my opinion, the shop functionality is better organized on Ko-fi.
As you can see on your screen.

What I like about Buy Me A Coffee, is that you can adjust “the coffee part”. So instead of a coffee, you can get creative and use tea, or banana...

And then you can also Generate it as a button.
With ko-fi you can only change the word “Coffee” to something else.. But that’s it.

01:33 Usability
They are both super easy to use and setting up a profile is quick and painless. If you have a Paypal account you can start receiving donations in minutes. 
Personally, I like the Ko-fi layout slightly better, but this is really my personal preference.

01:55 Fees
Now let’s talk about the biggest difference between the two… and it is how they charge fees.
So Ko-fi is totally free when using for one-time donations and they charge 5% for product sales and memberships.
If you upgrade to the Ko-fi Gold plan, then you pay a flat fee of six dollars per month,
And you don’t pay any other fees.

In contrast, buy me a coffee charges five percent for every donation or sale, but they don’t have monthly fees. 

So which one is cheaper? 
Well with quick math if you get donations or sell products for more than a hundred and twenty dollars a month. Then Ko-fi will be cheaper because you can switch to the flat fee.
For one-time donations, Ko-fi is cheaper.

02:44 Which is better Kofi or Buy me a coffee?

So which one do I think is better. Honestly, they are both really great services to get donations from supporters. And they both have pros and cons.

Personally, I needed a platform for one-time donations, and I like the logo of Ko-fi, look at it, just gives me a warm fuzzy feeling.

Hosting I'm using: https://punchsalad.com/hostpapa/ (60% discount)
Where I get my domains: https://punchsalad.com/namecheap-domain/
Email tool I use: https://punchsalad.com/lp/mailerlite/
WP plugins I'm using: https://punchsalad.com/recommendations?utm_campaign=PatreonAlt&utm_medium=social&utm_source=youtube
*Some of the above links might be affiliate links.
```

web.title: sponsoring some of the open source projects I use - Andrea Grandi
web.description: So, if you really care about open source, beware that GitHub stars can't be used to pay the bills please consider donating to the projects you ...
web.markdown:
After having thought about this for a while, I finally decided to start sponsoring some of the open source projects I regularly use.
I set aside a monthly budget and I evenly divided the amount across the projects.

## Who am I sponsoring?

So far I’ve started sponsoring these projects / people (in no particular order):

- [iTerm2](https://github.com/gnachman/iTerm2) \- a terminal for MacOS I’ve been using for years
- [Sebastián Ramírez](https://github.com/tiangolo) \- author of [FastAPI](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/)
- [Homebrew](https://github.com/Homebrew) \- a package manager for MacOS
- [Cryptomator](https://github.com/cryptomator) \- a tool to encrypt, decrypt and easily access files in various clouds
- [Pallets](https://github.com/pallets) \- they develop and maintain Flask, Click and a few other tools
- [Mastodon](https://github.com/mastodon) \- the social network I use most
- [Atuin](https://github.com/atuinsh) \- a shell history which supports a few different shells

## How much am I donating?

If you were expecting to find an amount, please move to the next paragraph 😅

What I can say is that everyone should donate an amount based on their own possibilities and estabilished budget. **If all the users** of the projects I mentioned, **donated** as much as I’m doing, **those developers would be rich** 😉

## How am I donating?

For my own simplicity, I decided to support only projects with a **Sponsor page on GitHub**. This way I can manage all the donations from a single website. I can decide to adjust the amounts and to sponsor more projects. I don’t want to discriminate anyone, but at the end of the day it’s up to me deciding who to support and how 🤷🏻‍♂️

## Could I do more?

Yes, but… there was a specific thing which prevented me from donating to other projects I wanted to sponsor. These projects (which I won’t mention, but I’ve contacted them in private to suggest considering a more flexible option) all **have a minimum amount set** in their Sponsor settings.

Honestly I can’t understand the reason for this, and I’m sure it’s not about fees, because [GitHub](https://docs.github.com/en/sponsors/sponsoring-open-source-contributors/about-sponsorships-fees-and-taxes#sponsorship-fees) takes a fixed fees percentage (3% credit card processing fee + 3% GitHub service processing fee) so receiving 1$ from 100 people or 100$ from 1 person doesn’t change the final amount of money the developer receives.

My humble suggestion is to **remove the minimum amount**: not everyone can or is willing to donate the minimum amount some of these projects have set and you will be able to collect more donations from more people.

## Conclusion

My personal opinion is that every cent counts. No matter if you can only afford 5$/month or if you are able to donate 100$/month: **every person should donate based on their own preferences and possibilities.**

I onestly would prefer to get 1$ from 100 different people than 100$ from a single person. A larger number of people believing in my projects is more important than the final amount.

So, if you really care about open source, beware that GitHub stars can’t be used to pay the bills 😉 **please consider donating** to the projects you love as much as you can!

web.markdown:
# Love what you do and make money too

Join all types of creators getting tips, memberships and sales from their fans!

[Get started](https://ko-fi.com/account/register "Sign Up")

## Payday your way

Decide how you want to earn, set your own terms and get paid directly. All from one place, at your own pace.


[Learn how Ko-fi works](https://ko-fi.com/features "Sign Up")

# Creators of all kinds, all kind!

From podcasts to cosplay, pixel art to crochet.

## The home of creative joy

When did churning out content, maximizing metrics and pushing for profit become standard?


At Ko-fi we don’t conform to that kind of ‘norm’.

Whether you’re making things and putting them out there or you’re showing the love for those that do – Ko-fi exists so that you can create, share, connect and support each other on your own terms.

Together we’re building a place where everyone can celebrate the joy of being free to explore your passions at your own pace.

[Read Ko-fi's story](https://ko-fi.com/our-story "Read Ko-fi's story")![Ko-fi story image, showing Ko-fi character playing game](https://storage.ko-fi.com/cdn/landing_assets/kofistory_character.png?quality=auto)

What is Ko-fi?

Ko-fi helps creators make an income from your biggest fans. Whether you’re an artist, streamer or any kind of creator you can accept tips, set up memberships, take requests and sell your work - all in one place.


How does Ko-fi work?

It’s simple! Create a free account, connect your own payment method (you get paid directly on Ko-fi), share it with your fans, and start earning in just a few minutes!


Ko-fi has everything you need to create, share and make money in one place and on your own terms.


Does Ko-fi take a fee?

There's no monthly fee so we only make money when you do. To cover our costs of running and building the service, Ko-fi takes a creator friendly 0-5% fee. [Learn more about our pricing here.](https://ko-fi.com/pricing)

Can I use Ko-fi if I’m just starting out?

Absolutely! Ko-fi is perfect for creators of all sizes. We're all about letting you do your thing, at your pace – no need to rush or churn out content to a schedule. Grow your support at your own pace without the pressure.


How do I get paid on Ko-fi?

You get paid directly and instantly into your own PayPal or Stripe account. Ko-fi never processes or holds funds, so no waiting around for payouts.


Supporters can pay you via PayPal, Cards, Apple Pay, Google Pay, Venmo, CashApp and loads of local payment methods.

They’re your fans so they’re your payments!

How is Ko-fi different from other services like Patreon?

Ko-fi puts creators first, giving you full control over how you connect with your fans.


You decide what to offer, set your own prices and terms and get paid directly and instantly through your own PayPal or Stripe account.


Unlike other sites, we don’t process transactions or control the relationship you have with your fans. We just give you the tools to help you grow.


![FAQs background image](https://storage.ko-fi.com/cdn/landing_assets/faq_character.png)

# Make money    doing what you love

ko-fi.com/

![Footer image](https://storage.ko-fi.com/cdn/landing_assets/footer_character.png?quality=auto)

# Ko-fi vs Buy Me a Coffee: Which One Truly Works for Creators in 2025?

[![Niabi Travel](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fill:32:32/1*ZdWsC7o3aFRpDp_aL5w_xw.jpeg)](https://medium.com/@niabitravel?source=post_page---byline--b1a9ab20853e---------------------------------------)

[Niabi Travel](https://medium.com/@niabitravel?source=post_page---byline--b1a9ab20853e---------------------------------------)

4 min read

·

May 21, 2025

--

[Listen](https://medium.com/m/signin?actionUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2Fplans%3Fdimension%3Dpost_audio_button%26postId%3Db1a9ab20853e&operation=register&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fmedium.com%2Fmr-plan-publication%2Fko-fi-vs-buy-me-a-coffee-which-one-truly-works-for-creators-in-2025-b1a9ab20853e&source=---header_actions--b1a9ab20853e---------------------post_audio_button------------------)

Share

Press enter or click to view image in full size

![Ko-fi vs Buy Me a Coffee](https://miro.medium.com/v2/resize:fit:700/0*x9d_Nw1QgANMgZ7Z)

Photo by [Nubelson Fernandes](https://unsplash.com/@nublson?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral) on [Unsplash](https://unsplash.com/?utm_source=medium&utm_medium=referral)

> You’re pouring your heart into your content — so why are platforms taking a cut? Here’s the real difference between Ko-fi and Buy Me a Coffee in 2025.

When you’re a creator — writer, artist, musician, or anyone building something online — you eventually face this question:

**How do I get paid directly by the people who love what I do?**

That’s where platforms like **Ko-fi** and **Buy Me a Coffee** come in. They promise a way to earn support without sponsors, ads, or algorithms. But which one is actually better?

After working with both and seeing what real creators go through, I’ll break down the real pros, cons, and what you should choose depending on where you are in your journey.

## First, Let’s Clear Something Up

Both Ko-fi and Buy Me a Coffee let you do the basics:

- Accept donations from supporters
- Set up monthly memberships
- Share updates or exclusive content

At first glance, they seem the same. But once you use them, the differences show — and they really matter depending on how you want to earn.

## Why Ko-fi Might Be the Better Long-Term Option

Ko-fi gives you more than just a link to accept tips. It offers tools that grow with you.

Here’s what you can do:

- Take one-time donations (with **0% fee**)
- Sell digital downloads like eBooks or templates
- Offer physical products or even commissions
- Build a paid membership area with tiers
- Customize your page with your own branding

And the best part? Ko-fi lets you keep all of your tips.

Even their free plan doesn’t take a cut from what supporters send you.

If you decide to upgrade to **Ko-fi Gold** (a few dollars a month), it removes platform fees from shop sales and gives you more flexibility. This makes it a smart choice for creators who want to grow over time.

## What Buy Me a Coffee Does Really Well

Now, if you just want to get started _fast_, Buy Me a Coffee is incredibly easy. The setup is quick, the design is clean, and you can be up and running in under 10 minutes.

You can:

- Collect tips from fans
- Create memberships
- Offer small digital “extras,” like wallpapers or PDFs

However, Buy Me a Coffee **takes 5% of all your earnings**, even from tips. That fee stays, no matter how much you make or what plan you’re on.

## Get Niabi Travel’s stories in your inbox

Join Medium for free to get updates from this writer.

Subscribe

Subscribe

Remember me for faster sign in

For creators who just want a no-fuss way to accept support and don’t mind giving up a small portion, it’s still a decent option.

## Who Helps You Keep More of Your Earnings?

Let’s talk numbers — because they matter.

- On **Ko-fi**, you get 100% of your tips. Nothing is taken out.
- On **Buy Me a Coffee**, 5% of every payment goes to the platform.

If you’re only receiving a few dollars here and there, this might not seem like a big deal. But over months — and especially if your support grows — that 5% can add up to a lot.

That makes Ko-fi a better deal for creators who want to keep more of what they earn.

## Discoverability: Can New Creators Get Found?

Ko-fi has a small edge here. They have an “Explore” section where creators can be found by people browsing the site. It’s not a huge traffic driver, but it does help some creators gain a little exposure.

Buy Me a Coffee doesn’t offer this. If you’re using it, you’ll need to bring in all of your traffic from social media, email, or your website.

## Easy of Use: Which One Is Smoother?

Here’s where Buy Me a Coffee shines. If you’re someone who just wants to set up and go, it’s a fantastic option. The layout is clean, and the entire setup process feels smooth.

Ko-fi takes a little more time, especially if you’re adding product listings, branding, or different income streams. But that extra effort gives you more control in the long run.

So it comes down to this:

- **Want to get started in minutes?** Go with Buy Me a Coffee.
- **Want more features and control over how you earn?** Go with Ko-fi.

## Final Thoughts: Which One Should You Pick?

Both platforms are good. But the right one depends on what you’re trying to build.

**Choose Ko-fi if:**

- You want to keep all your tips
- You’re planning to sell products or take commissions
- You need more freedom to customize and grow
- You see this as a long-term creative business

**Choose Buy Me a Coffee if:**

- You want something easy and fast to use
- You’re okay with a 5% fee
- You don’t need advanced features (yet)
- You’re testing the waters and want to get started quickly

> “You don’t have to be great to start, but you have to start to be great”.

## My Take?

I recommend **starting with Ko-fi** if you’re serious about your creative work. It gives you more ways to earn — and keeps more money in your pocket.

But if you just want a quick tip jar to get going and test your audience’s interest, **Buy Me a Coffee is perfectly fine** too.

The key is to just **start**. Don’t wait for perfect. Pick the one that fits your current energy and take that first step.
